title: [CI:DOCS] Man pages: Refactor common options: --dns
body:
Only between podman-build, create, and run. podman-pod-create
is too different.

As usual I went with the podman-run version. This means
keeping the word "flag" (which should be "option"), for
ease of review. I will fix in my in-progress cleanup PR.

For podman-build, I removed "during the build" and changed
it to a note for that man page only.

Signed-off-by: Ed Santiago <santiago@redhat.com>
```release-note
more man page deduplication
```

comments.body:
/hold

because I included a question in the commit message and would like to re-push with that question removed (and the text fixed accordingly)

I think you should add the comment about dns not being embedded in the image to the podman build man page.
I would add notes to all of the --dns-* options that they only effect the RUN commands within the build and will NOT be saved to the final image.


```
$ buildah build --dns-search redhat.com /tmp
STEP 1/2: FROM alpine
STEP 2/2: run cat /etc/resolv.conf
search redhat.com .
nameserver 10.0.2.3
nameserver 192.168.1.1
COMMIT
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 994393dc58e7 skipped: already exists  
Copying blob 52029398ae3f done  
Copying config 81b01c6e12 done  
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
--> 81b01c6e12c
81b01c6e12c02f3a0721b2572b017c504a447e02446057c2f0d9988580d6f992
$ podman run 81b01c6e12c02f3a0721b2572b017c504a447e02446057c2f0d9988580d6f992 cat /etc/resolv.conf
nameserver 10.0.2.3
nameserver 192.168.1.1
```
But then that text will also show up in podman-create and run man pages, where (I think?) it doesn't make much sense?

>I would add notes to all of the --dns-* options 

Oh.... I misunderstood. I interpreted this as "add notes to all the `options/dns-*.md` files; I now think you meant "add notes to all the `--dns-*` options in `podman-build.1.md.in` only. Is my new interpretation correct? Because that makes more sense.

Yes the comment above about saving in the image is only for podman-build man page.

This option cannot be combined with **--network** that is set to **none**. (Should be true for podman build as well.) 

Although it is not blocked right now.

 $ podman build --network=none --dns-search redhat.com /tmp
STEP 1/2: FROM alpine
STEP 2/2: run cat /etc/resolv.conf
--> Using cache 1134335726b341d4de3d8c165c56e3985c82db7202542d3cc77f92afafb7cb69
--> 1134335726b
1134335726b341d4de3d8c165c56e3985c82db7202542d3cc77f92afafb7cb69


I will add a block.

Added note to the podman-build page only. Updated commit message and github description.

/hold cancel

>This option cannot be combined with --network that is set to none. (Should be true for podman build as well.)

Yes, but the full text of that note is:
```
This option cannot be combined with **--network** that is set to **none** or **container:**_id_.
                                                                          ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
```
`podman build --network`, with autocompletion, offers me a `container` option but it is not documented in the man page itself. (Because I haven't deduped the `--network` option, because that's one of the horrible ones). Does the `container` option make sense for `podman build`, and if so, should I incorporate the full text of that note into the common `options/dns.md`?

I know this is probably a dumb question, and I'm really sorry to be taking up everyone's time on it. By doing this, I'm hoping to save us all time in the future.
No podman build --network container ...  Is not supported, as far as I know.
BTW I don't think this is a dumb discussion. I have already found a couple of issues in Buildah and Podman build about adding some incorrect combination of options.
Okay, thanks again. I've left the "cannot be combined" notes separate, then, with podman-build listing only `none` and podman-create and run listing `none` and `container`. Re-pushed.
